summary: Behavioral spec as single Markdown document parsed by state machine into typed objects
context: Phase 15 needed a machine-parseable behavioral specification covering all 32 MCP tools. The spec must be readable by humans (plugin authors) and parseable by the eval harness.
rationale: A single BEHAVIORS.md in plugin/ serves as both human documentation and eval input. A state-machine parser in test/eval/ transforms it into typed BehaviorSpec objects using shared Zod-validated interfaces. This avoids duplicating behavioral rules in code and keeps the spec authoritative.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Less human-readable, harder for plugin authors to maintain, would need separate documentation anyway
Couples spec to implementation language, not accessible to non-TS tooling, harder to review as documentation

alternatives.option: Multiple smaller spec files per tool category
alternatives.reason_rejected: Harder to enforce consistency across tools, more complex parser, no clear benefit for 32 tools
